name: Claude Opus 4.6
verdict: The gold standard for complex reasoning and long-form coherence. Unmatched where depth and nuance matter over latency. Quansynd's default for high-stakes intelligence tasks.

name: GPT-5
verdict: Formidable across all axes. Marginally trails Opus on contextual retention in dense multi-document tasks. An excellent default for versatile enterprise deployment at scale.

name: Gemini 2.5 Pro
verdict: Exceptional value-performance ratio. Leads mid-tier on contextual grasp and efficiency. Strong default for multimodal and search-augmented production workflows.

name: Claude Sonnet 4.6
verdict: The sweet spot. Near-Opus accuracy at significantly lower cost. Quansynd's production default for intelligent applications where both quality and throughput matter.

name: Grok 3
verdict: Standout creativity and real-time information access. Reliability inconsistencies make it unsuitable for mission-critical workflows. High ceiling - watch this space.

name: Mistral Large 3
verdict: The efficiency champion. Outstanding quality per token cost. Slight creative constraints, but highly reliable for structured analysis at scale.

name: Llama 4 Maverick
verdict: Open-source leader. Best for self-hosted deployments requiring efficiency. Accuracy gaps surface in ambiguous, multi-constraint problems.

name: DeepSeek R2
verdict: Disproportionately strong reasoning for its cost tier. An underrated analytical workhorse - creativity and reliability gaps hold it back.

name: Qwen 3 235B
verdict: Punches above its weight on multilingual tasks and efficiency. Context retention weakens significantly past 20k tokens.

name: GPT-4o mini
verdict: Deliberate capability trade-offs for edge deployment. Near-zero latency for lightweight tasks. The fastest viable option for simple classification workflows.
